source_rationale: RGS5 is a pericyte/perivascular marker, and its high expression in DDX4 AB+ cells suggests perivascular-like identity in this sorted population
TAGLN (transgelin) is a smooth muscle marker expressed in perivascular/stromal cells, indicating mesenchymal character
ACTA2 (alpha smooth muscle actin) is a canonical marker of smooth muscle and perivascular cells
MCAM (CD146) is a perivascular cell surface marker also expressed on some ovarian stromal progenitors
CD9 is a tetraspanin expressed on oocytes and germ cell-associated populations in the ovary
IGFBP7 is expressed in perivascular and stromal cells and regulates IGF signaling in the ovarian niche
Phospholamban is a smooth muscle/perivascular marker indicating contractile cell identity
Decorin is a proteoglycan highly expressed in ovarian stromal fibroblasts and is a canonical stromal marker
PDGFRA is a well-established marker of mesenchymal/stromal fibroblasts in the ovary
Collagen type I alpha 1 is a canonical extracellular matrix gene expressed by fibroblasts/stromal cells
SERPINE2 is a serine protease inhibitor expressed in ovarian stromal and granulosa-associated cells
Osteoglycin is a small leucine-rich proteoglycan characteristic of stromal fibroblasts
Midkine is a growth factor expressed in ovarian stromal cells involved in follicular development
Claudin-11 is a tight junction protein expressed in ovarian stromal/support cells
Von Willebrand factor is a canonical endothelial cell marker involved in hemostasis
Claudin-5 is a tight junction protein specific to endothelial cells and a widely used endothelial marker
EGFL7 is an endothelial-specific secreted factor critical for vascular development
SPARCL1 is highly expressed in endothelial cells and involved in vascular biology
GNG11 is a G-protein subunit enriched in endothelial cells and used as an endothelial marker
SDPR (CAVIN2) is involved in caveolae formation and is highly expressed in endothelial cells
Matrix Gla protein is expressed in endothelial and vascular smooth muscle cells
LIF receptor is expressed on endothelial cells and involved in vascular signaling
Anti-Müllerian hormone is a canonical marker of granulosa cells, produced by growing follicles
TNNI3 has been identified as a marker of granulosa cells in human ovarian tissue
WFDC2 (HE4) is expressed in granulosa cells and is a known ovarian epithelial/granulosa marker
Keratin 18 is an epithelial marker expressed in granulosa cells reflecting their epithelial character
Glutathione S-transferase alpha 1 is expressed in granulosa cells for detoxification during steroidogenesis
GATM is involved in creatine biosynthesis and is enriched in granulosa cells
HES1 is a Notch signaling target expressed in granulosa cells during folliculogenesis
EMX2 is a transcription factor expressed in granulosa cells important for ovarian development
Prostaglandin D2 synthase is highly expressed in granulosa cells and involved in ovarian function
BEX1 is enriched in granulosa cells and involved in cell cycle regulation
HLA-DRA is an MHC class II gene highly expressed on antigen-presenting immune cells
TYROBP (DAP12) is an adaptor protein essential for innate immune cell signaling in NK cells and myeloid cells
CCL5 (RANTES) is a chemokine produced by T cells and NK cells, a hallmark of immune activation
CXCR4 is a chemokine receptor broadly expressed on immune cells for homing and migration
CD69 is an early activation marker expressed on activated lymphocytes and NK cells
NKG7 is expressed in cytotoxic lymphocytes and NK cells, involved in granule-mediated cytotoxicity
HCST (DAP10) is an adaptor for NKG2D signaling in NK and T cells
C1QA is a complement component expressed by tissue-resident macrophages in the immune compartment
RGS1 regulates G-protein signaling in immune cells and is enriched in tissue-resident lymphocytes
Lysozyme is a canonical monocyte/macrophage marker involved in innate antimicrobial defense
CD163 is a scavenger receptor specific to monocytes and tissue-resident macrophages
AIF1 (IBA1) is a canonical monocyte/macrophage marker involved in phagocytosis
C1QA is a complement component highly expressed by monocytes and macrophages
C1QC is part of the complement C1q complex, a hallmark of monocyte-derived macrophages
C1QB completes the C1q trimer and is a robust marker of monocytes/macrophages
MS4A6A is a membrane-spanning protein enriched in monocytes and macrophages
MS4A7 is a monocyte/macrophage-specific membrane protein of the MS4A family
FGL2 (fibrinogen-like 2) is expressed by monocytes/macrophages and involved in immune regulation
IL1B is a pro-inflammatory cytokine produced by activated monocytes and macrophages
RNASE1 is secreted by monocytes/macrophages and involved in innate immune defense
Zona pellucida glycoprotein 3 is a canonical oocyte-specific marker essential for fertilization
FIGLA is an oocyte-specific transcription factor essential for folliculogenesis and oocyte development
KPNA7 is an oocyte-specific importin alpha involved in nuclear transport during oogenesis
STAG3 is a meiosis-specific cohesin subunit essential for oocyte chromosome segregation
UCHL1 (PGP9.5) is highly expressed in oocytes and used as an oocyte marker in ovarian tissue
KCNQ1-AS1 is a long non-coding RNA enriched in oocytes associated with imprinting regulation
Histone variant H2B is enriched in oocytes reflecting chromatin remodeling during oogenesis
RGS5 is a canonical pericyte marker regulating G-protein signaling in perivascular cells
Transgelin is a smooth muscle/pericyte marker indicating contractile perivascular identity
Alpha smooth muscle actin is a canonical marker of pericytes and vascular smooth muscle cells
Phospholamban is expressed in smooth muscle/perivascular cells regulating calcium handling
RERGL is enriched in perivascular/smooth muscle cells in the ovarian vasculature
NDUFA4L2 is a hypoxia-responsive gene enriched in pericytes in vascular niches
ADIRF is enriched in perivascular and smooth muscle cells
TIMP3 is a metalloproteinase inhibitor expressed by pericytes regulating extracellular matrix
Decorin is a canonical stromal fibroblast marker and extracellular matrix proteoglycan
PDGFRA is a well-established marker of mesenchymal stromal fibroblasts
Collagen type I is a major extracellular matrix component produced by stromal fibroblasts
Osteoglycin is a proteoglycan enriched in stromal fibroblasts
Complement C1s is produced by stromal fibroblasts as part of local complement production
Claudin-11 is expressed in ovarian stromal cells forming intercellular junctions
SERPINE2 is a serine protease inhibitor expressed by stromal fibroblasts regulating ECM remodeling
Complement factor H is produced by stromal cells as part of local immune regulation
HTRA1 is a serine protease expressed by stromal cells involved in extracellular matrix regulation
CCL5 (RANTES) is a chemokine highly expressed by effector T cells and NK cells
CD69 is an early T cell activation marker expressed on tissue-resident T cells
Granzyme K is expressed by CD8+ effector memory T cells and is a T cell cytotoxicity marker
IL32 is a pro-inflammatory cytokine predominantly produced by T cells and NK cells
PTPRC (CD45) is a pan-leukocyte marker essential for T cell receptor signaling
IL7R (CD127) is a canonical T cell marker essential for T cell survival and homeostasis
CD2 is a T cell surface glycoprotein involved in T cell activation and adhesion
NKG7 is expressed in cytotoxic T cells and NK cells, involved in granule exocytosis
Granzyme A is a serine protease in cytotoxic T lymphocytes mediating target cell apoptosis
CD52 is a glycoprotein highly expressed on T lymphocytes, the target of alemtuzumab
CCL4 (MIP-1β) is a chemokine produced by activated T cells during immune responses
Interferon gamma is the hallmark cytokine of Th1 and cytotoxic T cells
